# Ideographic Description Sequences (IDS) for CJK Unified Ideographs
# URL: <https://babelstone.co.uk/CJK/IDS.TXT>
# File Encoding: UTF-8 with BOM and CR/LF
# Maintained by: Andrew West (魏安) <babelstone@gmail.com>
# Unicode Version: 16.0 (September 2024)
# Total entries: 97,680
# File Date: 2025-06-27
#
#
# 1. This file is based on IDS data provided by Kawabata Taichi <https://github.com/kawabata/ids>, John Knightley,
#	and Andrew West, and from data provided in encoding submissions by IRG member bodies. However, the format of this
#	file was designed by myself, and the file does not reproduce the format or presentation form of any pre-existing IDS
#	data sources. I (Andrew West) have edited the original raw IDS data to conform to the format used in this file, and
#	have made a very large number of changes and corrections to the original data. My thanks to Eiso Chan (陈永聪), James
#	Kass, Huáng Jùnliàng (黄俊亮), Jaemin Chung, and Jerry You (游程宇) for providing numerous corrections and improvements
#	to the data; and my especial thanks to Michel Mariani who has provided thousands of corrections and improvements which
#	have immeasurably improved the quality of the IDS data.
#
# 2. Under U.S. copyright law, data (i.e. a collection of facts) is not eligible to copyright protection (see
#	<https://sco.library.emory.edu/research-data-management/publishing/copyright-data.html>), and as IDS descriptions
#	for CJK ideographs are facts not original creative compositions, IDS sequences in themselves are not eligible for
#	copyright protection (that is to say, you could no more copyright 彼 = ⿰彳皮 than you could copyright 1 + 1 = 2).
#	Therefore, anyone is free to make use of the IDS data provided in this file for personal or commercial purposes
#	without asking permission or providing attribution. I furthermore waive any copyright claims to the presentation
#	format of the IDS data used in this file, so anyone is free to fork the file, or use it as the basis for a new IDS
#	data set.
#
# 4. IDS sequences start with ^ and end with $. Some characters have more than one tab-separated IDS sequence,
#	corresponding to different source forms of the character, as described below.
#
# 5. IDS sequences on this page use only the following characters:
#	CJK Unified Ideographs (no CJK Compatibility Ideographs are used)
#	CJK Strokes (only where there is no corresponding CJK unified ideograph)
#	CJK Radicals Supplement (only if there is no corresponding CJK unified ideograph)
#	？ (fullwidth question mark) indicating an unrepresentable component
#	Ideographic Description Characters indicating relative placement (⿰⿱⿲⿳⿴⿵⿶⿷⿸⿹⿺⿼⿽)
#	⿻ = Overlay operator (binary): indicates that the two components overlay each other. As the relative positioning of
#		the overlay is unspecified, this operator may be ambiguous.
#	㇯ = Subtraction operator (binary): indicates that the stroke or component represented by the second operand is removed
#		from the character represented by the first operand, for example ㇯東㇒ = 𬻌. This is effectively the opposite of the
#		overlay operator (⿻), and like the overlay operator it may be ambiguous (e.g. ㇯心丶 for 𢖩 does not specify which of
#		the two dots is removed from 心).
#	⿾ = Mirror operator (unary): the operand is mirrored horizontally (e.g. ⿰⿾臣臣 = 臦).
#	⿿ = Rotation operator (unary): the operand is rotated 180° (e.g. ⿿了 = 𠄏).
#	〾 (Ideographic Variation Indicator): placed at the begining of an IDS sequence to indicate that one or more of the IDS
#		components differ in some unspecified minor way from the actual shape of the component given.
#	0..9 in curly brackets {} representing unencoded components (see below).
#
U+4E00	一	^一$(GHTJKPV)
U+4E16	世	^⿻廿𠃊$(GHTJKPV)
U+4E17	丗	^⿻廿丨$(GJ[K][U])	^⿱⿻卄丨一$(MT)	^⿱⿻卄丨一$(P)
U+4E91	云	^⿱二厶$(GHTJKPV)
U+4EA0	亠	^⿱丶一$(GHTKP)	^⿱丨一$(J[K])
U+53B6	厶	^⿰𠃋丶$(GHTJKP)
U+5EFF	廿	^⿱卄一$(GHTJKP)
U+5F03	弃	^⿱亠弁$(GTJKP)
U+672C	本	^⿻木一$(GHTJKPV)
U+6728	木	^⿻十𠆢$(GHTJKPV)
U+6797	林	^⿰木木$(GHTJKPV)
U+68C4	棄	^⿳亠厶⿻廿木$(GHTP)	^⿳亠厶⿱丗木$(JK)
